title: Pygmaeothamnus zeyheri
taxonomy: Pygmaeothamnus zeyheri is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the most diverse subfamilies within the Fabaceae family. The genus Pygmaeothamnus is a small genus of shrubs and small trees, with P. zeyheri being one of its two known species. P. zeyheri is native to the Western Cape province in South Africa, where it grows as a small shrub or tree. The classification of P. zeyheri reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships within the Fabaceae family.
